name: Salmon Steaks in Herb Blankets
recipeIngredient: Lemon thyme and tarragon (or herbs of your choice)
recipeInstructions:
Choose a gratin or baking dish that will accommodate the steaks side by side and use some of the butter to grease it well. Place the fish into the dish and season with a squeeze of lemon, a generous pinch of black pepper, and a little salt. Cover and set aside in a cool place until ready to cook.
To cook, dot the fish with the remaining butter, cover with foil, and bake at 375-400°F (190-200°C) for 20 to 25 minutes, or until the fish is hot and tender. While it cooks, chop coarsely and mix together a handful of aromatic herbs. Use approximately:
8 tablespoons parsley
6 tablespoons chives
4 tablespoons mint
2 teaspoons lemon thyme
2 teaspoons tarragon (or dill or fennel)
Scatter nearly half the herbs on a warm serving dish. Lay the fish steaks on a bed of herbs, scatter more herbs over the fish, spoon on the buttery cooking juices, and then add the remaining herbs. Serve with wedges of lemon and steamed new potatoes (other vegetables are best served as a separate course).
